description_long: This object consists of three vertically oriented, rectangular cabinet or shelf units of varying heights, all finished in a light gray, textured material resembling laminate or wood veneer. The tallest unit features multiple horizontal shelves, some of which contain visible red and black items, likely tools or utensils, suggesting its use for organized storage. The middle unit appears to have a large open compartment at the top and a closed section at the bottom, while the smallest unit is a simple, boxy structure with a closed front. The surfaces are flat and matte, with no visible handles or glass panels, giving the ensemble a minimalist, utilitarian appearance. The construction appears sturdy, suitable for urban environments such as garages, workshops, or public utility spaces. The open shelving and compartmentalized design indicate functionality for storing, organizing, and possibly securing various items.
description: A set of three gray, rectangular cabinets or shelves of different heights, with open and closed compartments, designed for organized storage in urban settings.
description_view_0: Front view showing all three units, with the tallest unit's open shelves and visible red and black items, and the closed fronts of the other two units.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth and thickness of the cabinets, with open tops visible and no contents exposed.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain backs of the cabinets, with no visible openings or features.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the solid sides and open tops, with no visible contents.
